disclaimer: Educational assessment. CARSI is an IICRC-approved CEC provider; this is CARSI-original Australian training, not IICRC certification.
note: Australian-produced. Grounded in the course's own modules. Founder to review before wiring live.

quizzes.title: Moisture, Mould, and Indoor Air Quality: Understanding the Link — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: According to the course, what is the critical factor that activates dormant mould spores and allows them to germinate and grow?
options: Moisture
Direct sunlight
Cold temperatures
Oxygen on its own

questions.questionText: The course states that indoor conditions become highly favourable for mould colonisation when sustained relative humidity consistently exceeds which level?
questions.sourceRef: M5 — The Role of Humidity and HVAC Systems

questions.questionText: Which instrument does the course describe as measuring the relative humidity of indoor air?
options: A hygrometer
A pin-type moisture meter
A thermal imaging camera
An air scrubber

questions.questionText: Why does the course say porous materials such as drywall and carpet often require removal rather than cleaning once they are colonised?
options: Because mould grows into the material, not just on the surface
Because such materials are always too expensive to clean
Because cleaning them always spreads spores to metal surfaces
Because they cannot absorb antimicrobial solutions
questions.sourceRef: M4 — Building Materials and Mould Susceptibility

questions.questionText: How does the course define MVOCs?
options: Microbial volatile organic compounds that are responsible for the musty, earthy odours associated with damp buildings
Mould-based vitamins that neutralise airborne spores
Mechanical ventilation output controls fitted to HVAC systems
Moisture vapour operating cycles used to schedule drying
questions.sourceRef: M3 — How Mould Affects Indoor Air Quality

questions.questionText: The course notes that short-term mould symptoms are often mistaken for seasonal allergies. What clue does it give that mould may be the underlying cause?
options: Symptoms such as sneezing, coughing and itchy eyes often improve when the person leaves the affected environment
Symptoms only ever appear at night, regardless of location
Symptoms are always accompanied by a high fever and skin infection
Symptoms never ease even after the person leaves the building
questions.sourceRef: M6 — Health Effects of Mould Exposure

questions.questionText: What does the course say about the need for air sampling when a mould problem is present?
options: Not all mould problems require air sampling; visible mould is often sufficient to warrant remediation
Air sampling is legally required before any remediation can begin
Air sampling removes the need for a visual inspection
Air sampling results never need to be interpreted by a professional
questions.sourceRef: M7 — Inspection Protocols and Air Sampling

questions.questionText: Why does the course warn against removing drying equipment too early?
options: Materials can reabsorb moisture or dormant mould spores can reactivate
The equipment is likely to overheat and fail
It automatically voids the equipment manufacturer's warranty
Air sampling cassettes can become contaminated by the fans
questions.sourceRef: M8 — Drying Strategies to Prevent Mould Regrowth
